subject: Chemistry
reference: JEE Main 2026 / National Testing Agency (NTA) / jeemain.nta.nic.in
notes: 30 questions | 120 marks | Section A: 20 MCQs, Section B: 10 Numericals (attempt any 5) | Divided into Physical, Inorganic, and Organic Chemistry

sub_topics: Importance and scope of chemistry
Laws of chemical combination — law of conservation of mass, law of definite proportions, law of multiple proportions
Dalton's atomic theory
Mole concept and molar mass
Atomic and molecular masses
Percentage composition and empirical and molecular formulas
Chemical reactions — stoichiometry and limiting reagents
Concentration terms — molarity, molality, normality, mole fraction
Discovery of electron, proton, and neutron
Thomson's model and Rutherford's nuclear model
Bohr's model — quantised energy levels and spectral lines
Quantum mechanical model — Heisenberg's uncertainty principle
Wave-particle duality — de Broglie relation
Quantum numbers — principal, azimuthal, magnetic, and spin
Aufbau principle, Hund's rule, and Pauli exclusion principle
Electron configurations of atoms and ions
Shapes of s, p, and d orbitals
System and surroundings — types of systems
First law of thermodynamics — internal energy and enthalpy
Enthalpy changes in chemical reactions — standard enthalpy of formation, combustion, and neutralisation
Hess's law of constant heat summation
Entropy and the second law of thermodynamics
Gibbs free energy — spontaneity and equilibrium
Relationship between ΔG, ΔH, ΔS, and temperature
Bond enthalpies and their applications
Dynamic nature of equilibrium
Equilibrium constant — Kc and Kp, relationship between them
Factors affecting equilibrium — Le Chatelier's principle
Reaction quotient Q and predicting direction of reaction
Applications of equilibrium constant
Degree of dissociation
Electrolytes and non-electrolytes
Ionisation of acids and bases — weak and strong acids/bases
Acid-base equilibria — Ostwald's dilution law
pH scale and pH calculations
Buffer solutions and their importance
Hydrolysis of salts and degree of hydrolysis
Solubility product constant (Ksp)
Common ion effect and its applications
Concept of oxidation and reduction
Oxidation number — rules and calculation
Balancing redox equations — oxidation number method and half-reaction method
Identifying oxidising and reducing agents
Applications of redox reactions in everyday life
Types of solutions — solute, solvent
Concentration units — molarity, molality, normality, mole fraction, ppm
Solubility — Henry's law
Vapour pressure of solutions — Raoult's law and its applications
Ideal and non-ideal solutions — positive and negative deviations
Colligative properties — relative lowering of vapour pressure, elevation of boiling point, depression of freezing point, osmotic pressure
van't Hoff factor and its significance
Abnormal molecular masses
Electrochemical cells — galvanic and electrolytic cells
Electrode potential and standard electrode potential
Nernst equation and its applications
Electrochemical series
Conductance in electrolytic solutions — specific, molar and equivalent conductance
Kohlrausch's law
Faraday's laws of electrolysis
Dry cell, lead acid battery, and fuel cells
Corrosion — causes and prevention
Rate of a chemical reaction — average and instantaneous rate
Factors affecting rate — concentration, temperature, catalyst
Order and molecularity of a reaction
Rate law and rate constant
Integrated rate equations — zero order and first order reactions
Half-life of zero and first order reactions
Collision theory of reaction rates
Arrhenius equation — activation energy and frequency factor
Effect of catalyst on activation energy

major_topics.topic_name: Periodic Table and Periodicity in Properties
sub_topics: Historical development — Mendeleev's and modern periodic law
Electronic configuration and periodic table
Types of elements — s, p, d, and f block elements
Periodic trends — atomic and ionic radii
Periodic trends — ionisation enthalpy
Periodic trends — electron gain enthalpy
Periodic trends — electronegativity
Valence, oxidation states, and diagonal relationships
Kossel-Lewis approach to chemical bonding
Ionic bond — lattice energy and Born-Haber cycle
Covalent bond — Lewis structures and formal charge
Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) theory
Valence bond theory — hybridisation (sp, sp2, sp3, sp3d, sp3d2)
Molecular orbital theory — bonding, antibonding MOs, bond order
MO diagrams of homonuclear diatomic molecules (H2, N2, O2, F2)
Resonance and its significance
Polarity of bonds and molecules — dipole moment
Intermolecular forces — hydrogen bonding, van der Waals forces
Group 13 elements — physical and chemical properties, uses and compounds of boron (borax, boric acid, diborane)
Group 14 elements — properties, allotropes of carbon, compounds of silicon and carbon
Group 15 elements — properties and compounds of nitrogen (ammonia, oxides of nitrogen, nitric acid), phosphorus allotropes and compounds
Group 16 elements — properties and compounds of oxygen and sulphur (H2S, SO2, SO3, H2SO4, ozone)
Group 17 elements (Halogens) — properties, compounds of fluorine, chlorine, and interhalogen compounds
Group 18 elements (Noble gases) — properties and uses
General introduction and electronic configuration of d-block elements
Physical and chemical properties of transition metals
Variable oxidation states and colour of transition metal compounds
Magnetic properties and catalytic behaviour
Important compounds — KMnO4, K2Cr2O7 (preparation, properties, and uses)
Lanthanides — electronic configuration and lanthanide contraction
Actinides — electronic configuration and comparison with lanthanides
Coordination compounds — ligands, central atom, coordination number
IUPAC nomenclature of coordination compounds
Isomerism — structural and stereoisomerism in coordination compounds
Bonding in coordination compounds — Werner's theory, VBT, and CFT (introductory)
Colour and magnetic properties of coordination compounds
Importance and applications of coordination compounds
Environmental Chemistry — environmental pollution (air, water, soil)
Atmospheric reactions — greenhouse effect and global warming
Acid rain — causes and effects
Ozone depletion — causes and effects
Industrial waste and its management
Tetravalency of carbon and catenation
Structural representations — structural, condensed, and bond-line formulas
Classification of organic compounds
IUPAC nomenclature — rules and applications
Isomerism — structural and stereoisomerism (optical and geometric)
Electronic displacement effects — inductive, resonance, hyperconjugation
Electrophiles and nucleophiles
Types of organic reactions — substitution, addition, elimination
Reaction intermediates — carbocations, carbanions, free radicals
Purification techniques — distillation, crystallisation, sublimation, chromatography
Alkanes — nomenclature, isomerism, conformations, physical and chemical properties
Alkenes — nomenclature, isomerism, structure, methods of preparation, chemical reactions
Alkynes — nomenclature, isomerism, structure, acidic character, preparation and reactions
Aromatic hydrocarbons — benzene, structure and aromaticity
Electrophilic aromatic substitution reactions
Directive influence of substituents
Carcinogenicity and toxicity of aromatic compounds
Nomenclature and physical properties of aldehydes and ketones
Preparation of aldehydes and ketones
Nucleophilic addition reactions — mechanisms
Reactivity of carbonyl group
Aldol condensation and Cannizzaro reaction
Oxidation and reduction of aldehydes and ketones
Nomenclature, preparation, and physical properties of carboxylic acids
Chemical reactions of carboxylic acids — acidity, esterification
Formic acid and acetic acid — special properties
Amines — nomenclature, classification, and physical properties
Preparation and chemical reactions of amines
Diazonium salts — preparation and synthetic applications
Cyanides and isocyanides
Amides — preparation and hydrolysis
Carbohydrates — classification, structure of glucose and fructose
Reducing and non-reducing sugars
Disaccharides and polysaccharides — sucrose, starch, cellulose, glycogen
Proteins — amino acids, peptide bond, polypeptides, and proteins
Structure of proteins — primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary
Denaturation of proteins
Enzymes — nature and mechanism of enzyme action
Nucleic acids — DNA and RNA structure
Biological functions of nucleic acids
Vitamins — classification and functions
Hormones — general introduction
Chemicals in medicines — analgesics, tranquilisers, antiseptics, antibiotics, antifertility drugs
Chemicals in food — preservatives, artificial sweeteners
Cleansing agents — soaps and detergents, mechanism of cleansing action
